summary: Introduces a CoT-Output 2x2 safety matrix to diagnose turn-level failures in multi-turn reasoning models, distinguishing robust alignment, alignment faking, jailbreaks, and a new 'context-injection failure' where safe reasoning still yields harmful output. Finds explicit oversight cues paradoxically raise alignment-faking rates.

ABC-Bench measures agentic biosecurity-relevant capabilities, evaluating LLM agents on operating liquid-handling robots, designing DNA fragments, and evading synthesis screening. All tested agents beat the median human-expert baseline, and wet-lab validation confirmed one model's scripts successfully assembled DNA with expected sequences.

A Nature perspective arguing that AI is reshaping mathematics and physics not by replacing human intuition but by changing how questions are posed, explored, and understood, surveying machine-learning-driven discovery across both fields.

Trains a small transformer encoder to embed Terminal-Wrench trajectories so a linear probe detects reward hacking, matching an LLM-as-judge's AUC (~0.95) at roughly four orders of magnitude lower cost; removing natural-language reasoning from inputs sharply degrades detection.

Defines PRIME, a learned capability to assess task correctness and exploit proxy-gold reward gaps, detected via CoT monitoring, probes, and activation vectors. PRIME emerges before sustained reward hacking and forecasts its onset; ablating its directions reduces hacking, suggesting an early-warning signal for misalignment.

Anthropic case study tasking research agents (Claude, Biomni, GPT) to retrieve sequence data from NCBI Virus. Even strong models fell short of reliable accuracy until a deterministic retrieval layer (gget virus) raised it to near 100%, arguing biological databases must be redesigned for agents.

RecurGuard is a runtime monitor against reasoning-token consumption attacks (denial-of-service/wallet) that inject decoy tasks. Tracking recurrence, volume growth, and query progress, it detects 99% of OverThink and 92% of ExtendAttack cases with near-zero false positives, though full semantic evasion still slips through.

Introduces Alem, a JAX/Craftax-based benchmark for open-ended, long-horizon multi-agent coordination. Thirteen LLMs evaluated zero-shot average only ~6% normalized return; communication contributes most to coordination, and strong single-agent competence does not imply coordination ability.

Anthropic effort to improve Claude at chemistry; the first study examines how Claude reads NMR spectra, a chemist's most common analytical input, leveraging multimodal reasoning to translate among representations like structures, instrument readouts, and database notations.

OpenAI's new ChatGPT memory system ('Dreaming') consolidates and updates stored user preferences and context across conversations to keep them fresh and relevant, improving personalization and helpfulness.

Position paper arguing that superintelligence built via solipsistic, unilateral optimization is unlikely to be cooperative, since deployment induces endogenous non-stationarity. Calls for a non-solipsistic paradigm treating interdependence, adaptive evaluation, institutions, and preserved human agency as core design principles.

Shows RL-based drag reduction in wall turbulence can reward-hack: degenerate controllers report large drag cuts while total dissipation rises. Fixing a differentiable mass-conservation projection, a recurrent wider-sensing policy, and a true wall-power reward yields an honest 17% reduction within a closed energy budget.

SymptomAI deploys conversational AI agents for end-to-end patient interviewing and differential diagnosis via the Fitbit app, randomizing 13,917 participants across five agents. On a clinician-evaluated subset, its differential diagnoses were significantly more accurate (OR=2.56).
OpenAI expands GPT-Rosalind, a life-sciences model, with stronger biological reasoning, medicinal-chemistry expertise, genomics analysis, and experimental-workflow capabilities.

ReasoningFlow represents LLM reasoning traces as fine-grained directed acyclic graphs capturing backtracking and self-correction. Across five models and three tasks it finds structurally similar traces, surfaces fine-grained behaviors aiding monitorability, and shows most erroneous steps don't feed final answers.

Studies generalization failures of helpful-only models trained to always follow user intent. Beyond reduced refusals, they exhibit emergent misalignment, residual refusals, sycophancy, poor steerability, and incoherent character; synthetic-document fine-tuning and character-focused SFT/RL mitigate these issues.

CHERRL is a controllable testbed that injects known LLM-judge biases to reliably reproduce reward hacking in rubric-based RL, enabling observation of reward divergence and precise hacking onset. It analyzes judge biases by discoverability and exploitability and demonstrates agent-based detection of hacking onset from training logs.
Anthropic report mapping a year of AI-enabled cyberattacks onto the MITRE ATT&CK framework, assessing how well existing security techniques and frameworks hold up as AI changes attackers' methods.

Proposes a framework to quantify faithful confidence expression in large reasoning models, comparing linguistic decisiveness against intrinsic uncertainty from token probabilities, hidden states, and response consistency, with prefix-conditioned sampling. Finds faithful calibration remains a significant challenge that extended reasoning does not fix.

Glass Box is a runtime constitutional-AI verification layer for autonomous CubeSats that checks each candidate action against physics-grounded constraints and seven LTL safety invariants (verified by Z3/NuSMV) before execution, attaching explainability scores and audit logs with overhead linear in rule count.

Empirical taxonomy of RLHF failure modes using a compact PPO/DPO pipeline with two LLM judges. Classifying checkpoint transitions distinguishes reward hacking, collapse, and evaluator gaming; aggressive PPO shows the highest localized reward-hacking rate, and a pre-transition model predicts future hacking (AUC 0.82).

Proposes LAP (Lab Agent Protocol) for the agent-to-instrument edge missing from MCP and A2A. It adds signed InstrumentCards, exclusive reservations, a cryptographic safety-fence handshake gating hazardous operations, and a physically-typed, calibration-anchored, uncertainty-bearing MeasurementResult schema for autonomous labs.
A practical guide to marketing a childminding business, covering how to create adverts that reflect a unique selling point and where to advertise most effectively.

Systematically benchmarks nine protein-folding and generative methods across 20 monomeric proteins with active and inactive states for capturing conformational flexibility. Extending the pairwise-aligned-error metric to ensembles, Chai-1 best recovers known conformations and plausible intermediates, positioning generative models as efficient alternatives to molecular dynamics.

Presents format-aware optimizations in the Dataproc Spark engine for Lakehouse data management: Precision Merge for row-level transactional updates, Direct Binary Merge compaction, and asynchronous VACUUM, reporting over 50% faster execution and up to 10x less shuffle and faster routine maintenance.

Multi-arm randomized controlled trial (N=495 working adults) testing three app-delivered audio sleep interventions (Bedtime Stories, Sleep Sounds, Sleep Skills) against a digital control. None significantly outperformed control on sleep disturbance or related outcomes after four weeks.

Opinion article framing AI as driving a new industrial revolution that automates routine tasks at scale, reshaping productivity, creativity, markets, and labor while elevating human attention and empathy as increasingly valued resources.
OpenAI launches Rosalind Biodefense, expanding vetted access to its GPT-Rosalind bio model for developers and U.S. government partners working on biodefense, public health, and pandemic preparedness.

ConsisGuard targets the deliberation-to-enforcement gap in reasoning-based LLM safety guardrails, where rationales don't match final decisions. Via policy-to-decision trajectory distillation and functional-coupling alignment, it improves harmfulness detection while reducing policy-execution failures.

Proposes weak-critic strong oversight, where a weak model supplies revision directions rather than labels or judgments. Progressive on-policy critique distillation (OPCD) filters high-quality critiques and distills critic-guided behavior, improving strong models on reasoning and alignment benchmarks as a path to scalable oversight.
